app.name: NWK
app.tagline: A safety net for travelers in Korea

home.headline: Before you exchange — real Korean prices.

bagaji.headline: How much tourists overpaid yesterday — exposed.
bagaji.sub: Cases verified by our price-check tool. We show exactly how much was paid over the fair range.

home.kitLinkTitle: Korea kit — first 30 min, ATM, connectivity, emergency
home.coursesLinkTitle: Bucheon themed courses — by style and duration
home.coursesSectionTitle: Recommended courses by style and duration
home.rightNowLabel: Real Korean prices · Travel without rip-offs
home.pricesFootnote: Bucheon-si public data · 37 administrative dong avg · FX ₩1,330/USD

arex.title: AREX Airport Express
arex.detail: ICN → Seoul Station, 43 min · 06:30–22:50 · reserved seat

tmoney.title: T-money transit card
tmoney.detail: Buy & top up at any convenience store · works on subway, bus, some taxis

helpline.title: 1330 Tourist Hotline
helpline.detail: 24/7 · EN, JA, ZH, RU, VI · free translation, directions and complaints

atm.title: Airport & hotel ATMs
atm.body: ₩3,500–5,000 per withdrawal. CU/GS25 Global ATMs are around ₩1,000.

taxi.title: Short-trip taxi refusals
taxi.body: Under 3 km is often refused. Kakao T app locks the fare and prevents refusals.

guide.title: Fake guides in Myeongdong/Insadong
guide.body: "Free tour" approach → shop steering + tip demands. Official guides wear orange vests.

home.matchedTitle: What foreign travelers are picking right now
home.sourceBody: KTO TourAPI · MOLIT VWorld · KMA · BOK. No ads, no commissions, no sponsors.
home.exploreMapTitle: Explore 500+ Bucheon-area spots on a clustered map

search.title: Find a place
search.subhead: Search tourist sites across Korea via Korea Tourism Organization's public data. Tap any result for the etiquette, pricing and safety tips that apply there.
search.mockNotice: Showing demo data. Live results will appear once a TourAPI key is configured.
search.emptyHint: Search by city, sight, or keyword
search.tapPrompt: Tap a pin on the map
search.tapHint: Zoom in until individual pins appear, then tap any of them. The matched info for that place appears right here without leaving the map.
search.noAdvisory: No curated tips for this place yet. You can still get a verdict in the Price Verdict tab.
search.noResultsHint: Try another keyword or switch the filter to All
search.emptyViewport: Zoom out to see more spots
search.geoEmpty: No attractions within 3 km. Move to a different area and try again.

map.title: Map
map.subhead: Discover places and routes around you.

about.title: NWK — No Worries Korea
about.tagline: A safety net for travelers in Korea.

contest.label: Contest entry
contest.title: 2026 Korean Tourism Data Contest submission
contest.body: Built on KTO TourAPI and Bucheon City's open data, NWK pairs LIVE fair-price checks with 31 themed Bucheon itineraries in four languages.

pipeline.label: Data pipeline
pipeline.title: Public data, wired into the app

prices.name: Bucheon City personal-service fees
prices.body: Min / max / sample counts pulled LIVE per item.

community.name: Traveler participation
community.body: Likes and notes feed back into course updates and tourism-product ideas.

m3.title: Admin CMS launch
m3.body: Tourism-product managers will edit courses and moderate notes directly from an admin console.

m6.title: Scale to 15 regional metros
m6.body: Onboard each metro's personal-service-fee dataset with region-specific editor seats.

m12.title: AI course recommendations
m12.body: Personalized picks and a multilingual Q&A bot powered by accumulated notes and likes.

install.title: Add to home screen
install.body: Install NWK for instant launch and offline access to places you've recently viewed.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.body: Your location stays on your device and is never sent to our servers. Sign-in collects only your email and display name.

dataSources.label: Data sources
dataSources.title: Built on Korea's public tourism data

tour.name: KTO TourAPI
tour.body: All tourism POI search, nearby lookups and detail pages pull from the Korea Tourism Organization's public TourAPI (KorService2/EngService2/JpnService2/ChsService2) on data.go.kr. Content is delivered in your selected language.
tour.meta: data.go.kr · Entry to the Public Tourism Data Contest

kakao.name: Kakao Maps
kakao.body: Map rendering only — normal + satellite views. No POI data; Kakao is a visualization layer.

prices.name: Fair-price benchmarks
prices.body: We pull Bucheon City's personal-service fee survey (published on Korea's public data portal) and compare every price you enter against its LIVE min/max range. Each verdict shows the sample count and survey date, and we're rolling the same pipeline out to 15 provincial datasets next.

gate.signInBody: Sign in with a registered admin account to manage courses and notes.
gate.deniedBody: Share the uid below with an operator to request the admin claim.

links.notesDesc: Review traveler notes across all courses

pipeline.title: {{pois}} TourAPI POIs · {{core}} Bucheon core
pipeline.body: The app consumes TourAPI's 5 endpoints across 4 language services as required by the contest. A custom translation registry fills gaps the TourAPI services don't translate.

courses.title: Courses
courses.help: Toggle publish state on {{n}} courses. A toggle creates a Firestore document for that course; subsequent edits in Firestore override the static JSON.

check.title: Let's check your travel bill
check.subhead: We'll compare it against Korea's real average prices in a few taps.

safeTransit.title: Transit fares, sanity-checked
safeTransit.body: Subway and bus run on fixed T-money fares. Taxis, airport buses, KTX and car rentals price by distance, meter or route — see the fair range for every mode.

form.taxiContext: Taxi is computed from distance and time

advice.careful: 15–40% above average. Worth checking a second place.
advice.bagaji: More than 40% over the fair range. You can report it to 1330 (24/7 multilingual).

check.sourceNote: Bucheon personal-service fee survey · LIVE from Korea's public data portal

courses.title: Bucheon — Visit Like This
courses.subtitle: Themed travel courses centered on Bucheon — from half-day strolls to 3-day stays, pick your style.
courses.sourceBody: POIs · Korea Tourism Organization   |   Prices · Bucheon city public data

course.notesProposalBody: The opinions you share here feed our tourism product research and help us refine the course. We don't store personally identifiable information.
course.notePlaceholder: Share a tip or impression as an opinion on this course
course.noteError: Couldn't post your opinion. Please try again in a moment.
course.noteLoginPrompt: Sign in to share an opinion.
course.notesEmpty: No opinions yet. Be the first to share one.

kit.title: Korea survival kit
kit.subhead: The numbers, hotlines and habits foreigners actually need from landing onward.

sectionSubs.arrival: Your landing-to-city checklist — transit, immigration, customs and the taxi app.
sectionSubs.money: How to pay, exchange, and get VAT back — spend like a local.
sectionSubs.connect: Get online the moment you land and the apps Koreans actually use daily.
sectionSubs.phrases: 20 phrases with romanized pronunciation — use them at restaurants, shops and on the street.
sectionSubs.emergency: 24/7 multilingual hotlines and the foreigner-only help numbers.

arex.title: AREX Airport Express
arex.body: ICN → Seoul Station, 43 min · 06:30–22:50 · reserved seat

tmoney.title: T-money transit card
tmoney.body: Buy/charge at any convenience store · subway, bus, some taxis

immigration.title: Foreigner Immigration
immigration.body: K-ETA pre-registration skips lines · Smart Entry (KISS) for age 9+ residents

customs.title: Customs allowance
customs.body: USD 800 duty-free per person · 1 L alcohol, 200 cigarettes · declare if over

kakaoT.title: KakaoT taxi app
kakaoT.body: Foreign card payment · choose Regular/Black/Premium · avoid meter scams

atmGlobal.title: CU/GS25 Global ATM
atmGlobal.body: Cheapest foreign-card withdrawals. Open 24/7 at every convenience store.

atmAirport.title: Airport & hotel ATMs
atmAirport.body: Convenient but 3–5× the fee. Use only in a pinch.

cardFirst.title: Card first, cash as backup
cardFirst.body: Almost every shop takes cards. Cash only needed in traditional markets.

exchangeMyeongdong.title: Myeongdong private booths
exchangeMyeongdong.body: 10–20 KRW/USD better than banks. Bring your passport.

wowpass.title: WOWPASS (foreigner card)
wowpass.body: Exchange + T-money combo · passport only, no Korean ID · Myeongdong/Hongdae/airport

taxRefund.title: Tax refund (VAT)
taxRefund.body: 10–15% back on purchases over ₩30,000 · get customs stamp then claim at airport

tipCulture.title: No tipping culture
tipCulture.body: Restaurants, taxis, hotels — no tips expected. Only 5-star concierge is an exception.

esimKt.title: KT roaming eSIM
esimKt.body: 15 GB / 30 days. QR activation in under 10 minutes.

sim5day.title: 5-day unlimited SIM
sim5day.body: ICN arrivals floor, SKT/KT counters. Passport required.

wifiPublic.title: Public Wi-Fi
wifiPublic.body: Free on subway, buses and cafes. Look for 'Public WiFi Free' SSID.

kakaoTalk.title: KakaoTalk (essential)
kakaoTalk.body: 99% of Koreans use it · bookings, business chat, IDs instead of numbers

naverMap.title: Naver / Kakao Map
naverMap.body: More accurate than Google in Korea · live transit & taxi fares

papago.title: Papago translator
papago.body: Naver's translation app · camera, voice, chat · tuned for Korean

lostItem.title: Lost & Found Center
lostItem.body: 182 · search subway, taxi and public-transit lost items

humanRights.title: Foreigner Info Center
humanRights.body: 1345 · Ministry of Justice · 18 languages · rights & status help

phrases.hello: Hello — polite greeting when meeting someone or entering a shop.
phrases.thanks: Thank you — formal, works in every situation.
phrases.sorry: Sorry / excuse me — also used when squeezing past someone.
phrases.howMuch: How much is it? — essential at markets and street stalls.
phrases.tooExpensive: That's too expensive — signals you want to haggle.
phrases.cheaper: Could you give me a discount? — works at markets, not chain stores.
phrases.english: Do you speak English? — ask staff or passers-by for help.
phrases.help: Please help me — lost, unwell, or need assistance.
phrases.water: Can I have some water? — always free at restaurants.
phrases.notSpicy: Not spicy, please — tone down a dish.
phrases.check: Check, please — ask for the bill at a restaurant.
phrases.takeOut: To-go, please — pack up leftovers or drinks.
phrases.receipt: Can I get a receipt? — needed for the VAT refund.
phrases.wifiPw: What's the Wi-Fi password? — at cafes and lounges.
phrases.oneMore: One more, please — refills or additional orders.
phrases.here: Over here! — how you politely flag down a server in Korea.
phrases.delicious: This is delicious — a compliment that goes a long way.

notFound.title: You're off the map
notFound.body: The page you were looking for has moved or doesn't exist. Head back to the home screen to get your bearings.

profile.title: Me
profile.guestHint: Sign in to save favorites and history

price.water.title: Bottled water
price.water.body: 500ml at a convenience store. Tap water is drinkable but many locals still prefer bottled.

price.coffee.title: Cafe coffee
price.coffee.body: A regular Americano at a chain cafe. Specialty cafes run higher.

price.meal.title: Casual meal
price.meal.body: Average per-person meal at a local restaurant outside tourist zones.

price.taxi_base.title: Taxi base fare
price.taxi_base.body: Daytime base fare in Seoul. Late-night rides add roughly 20%.

price.subway_base.title: Subway base fare
price.subway_base.body: One ride with a T-money card. Cash adds about 100 KRW.

transit.tmoney.title: Get a T-money card
transit.tmoney.body: Rechargeable transit card sold at any convenience store. Works on subway, bus, and some taxis nationwide.

transit.subway_seoul.title: Seoul Metro
transit.subway_seoul.body: Nine numbered lines plus extensions. Most stations have English signage and free Wi-Fi.

transit.subway_busan.title: Busan Metro
transit.subway_busan.body: Four lines covering central Busan. T-money works here too.

transit.ktx.title: KTX high-speed rail
transit.ktx.body: Seoul to Busan in about 2h 40m. Book via the Korail app or at the station.

transit.taxi_app.title: Kakao T for taxis
transit.taxi_app.body: The most common taxi-hailing app. English interface and card payment supported.

etiquette.shoes_off.title: Remove shoes indoors
etiquette.shoes_off.body: Expected in hanok stays, temples, and many traditional restaurants. Look for a raised floor or shoe rack.

etiquette.bow.title: A slight bow is polite
etiquette.bow.body: A small nod when greeting or thanking someone is standard. Handshakes are fine too.

etiquette.no_tipping.title: No tipping culture
etiquette.no_tipping.body: Service charge is included. Cash tips can confuse staff.

etiquette.palace_respect.title: Palace grounds
etiquette.palace_respect.body: Stay on marked paths, no drones, and keep voices low inside preserved halls.

safety.emergency.title: Emergency numbers
safety.emergency.body: 112 police, 119 fire and ambulance. English support is available 24/7.

safety.tap_water.title: Tap water is safe
safety.tap_water.body: Seoul tap water meets WHO standards, though many travelers still prefer bottled.

safety.crowds.title: Watch for crowds
safety.crowds.body: Markets and shopping streets get dense on weekends. Keep bags in front of you.
